subject: Ecology
title: Carrying Capacity
description: A micro-lesson that makes Carrying Capacity usable.

hook.text: Every environment can only support so many individuals. That ceiling is the carrying capacity.

buildup.text: As a population grows, food shrinks, disease spreads, and space gets scarce.

discovery.text: Growth slows and levels off, forming an S-shaped curve called logistic growth.

twist.text: Overshoot the carrying capacity and a crash follows—sometimes devastating the population.

climax.text: Carrying capacity isn't fixed. A drought or flood can lower it overnight.

punchline.text: Every environment has a guest limit.

quiz.question: What happens when a population overshoots carrying capacity?
